report:
Ecolytix Technologies – Private Equity Investment Memo
Date: June 14, 2026

I. Company Overview and Business Model

– Ecolytix Technologies Ltd. is a UK-based cleantech startup specializing in advanced industrial wastewater treatment using proprietary bio-electrochemical reactors (BERs).
– Founded in 2018 with HQ in Cambridge, UK, the company commercialized its first product, the "EcoBER-200" system, in 2021.
– Ecolytix targets large manufacturing clients in sectors including chemicals, food & beverage, and pharmaceuticals that face stringent environmental regulations.
– Revenue streams:
  • Equipment sales of modular BER units customized per client needs.
  • Recurring revenue from remote monitoring & maintenance contracts.
  • Licensing fees from patented bio-catalyst strains used in reactors.
– Business model emphasizes long-term partnerships with clients, with warranties and performance guarantees.

II. Technology and IP

– Core technology: Bio-electrochemical reactors (BERs) that harness electroactive bacteria to degrade organic pollutants while generating small amounts of electricity, reducing energy costs.
– IP portfolio includes 12 granted patents worldwide covering reactor design, bio-catalyst formulations, and digital control algorithms.
– Distinctive feature: modular, scalable units reducing installation downtime and allowing retrofits.
– Digital platform "EcoInsight" enables real-time wastewater quality tracking and remote optimization.

III. Market Landscape

– Global industrial wastewater treatment market projected CAGR 7.5% (2025–2030), reaching $32B by 2030. Increasing regulatory pressure drives demand for innovative, energy-efficient solutions.
– Ecolytix currently operates primarily in Europe (UK, Germany, France) with entry into North America underway.
– Competitors:
  • ClearFlow Solutions – Established player in membrane bioreactors.
  • Aquaclean Tech – Mid-sized firm with chemical oxidation solutions.
  • GreenWave Systems – Early-stage startup in microbial fuel cells.
– Ecolytix is positioned as a technology innovator with lower energy use and flexible modularity.

IV. Customers and Contracts

– 25 active contracts as of Q1 2026, including:
  • ChemPro Manufacturing (Germany): €3.6M contract for a BER system serving 24/7 plant operations.
  • VitaFoods Ltd. (UK): €2.1M installation plus annual €0.3M monitoring service.
  • PharmaPure (France): multi-phase project valued at €5.4M over 3 years.
– Contract terms typically span 5–10 years for maintenance.
– Customer satisfaction surveys indicate strong retention (95%) and willingness to upgrade.

V. Financial Summary (Audited, € Million)

| Metric                 | 2022   | 2023   | 2024   | 2025(E) | 2026(F) |
|------------------------|--------|--------|--------|---------|---------|
| Revenue                | 8.2    | 14.7   | 26.4   | 42.1    | 61.7    |
| Gross Margin (%)       | 54.3   | 57.8   | 60.1   | 61.5    | 62.7    |
| EBITDA                 | -3.1   | 0.7    | 5.2    | 11.9    | 18.5    |
| EBITDA Margin (%)      | -37.8  | 4.8    | 19.7   | 28.2    | 30.0    |
| Net Income             | -5.4   | -1.1   | 1.8    | 7.2     | 12.0    |
| CapEx                  | 2.2    | 3.0    | 4.5    | 5.0     | 7.0     |
| Net Debt (Cash)        | 6.8    | 5.2    | 1.3    | -3.2    | -9.0    |

VI. Growth Drivers

– Strong double-digit revenue growth (avg. 64% CAGR 2022–2026 forecasted) driven by:
  • Increasing customer pipeline and expanding adoption in new geographies.
  • Recurring revenue mix rising from 15% (2024) to expected 25% (2026) due to maintenance and licensing.
  • Product pipeline includes larger capacity BER units and upgraded catalysts.
– Environmental legislation tightening accelerates demand for advanced treatment.
– Digital platform adoption enables improved operational efficiency and client retention.

VII. Risks and Challenges

– Technology execution risk: scale-up of new product variants may encounter delays.
– Customer concentration: Top 5 customers represent ~50% of 2025 revenue.
– Regulatory risks: changes in environmental policies could impact demand dynamics.
– Competition from emerging technologies (e.g., advanced oxidation) remains a threat.

VIII. Recent Developments

– Closed €15M Series C funding in Q4 2025 from strategic investors.
– Established US office in Boston in Q1 2026; signed LOIs with 3 North American industrial clients.
– Partnership formed with WaterNet Analytics for enhanced data integration.

IX. Summary

Ecolytix Technologies is a high-growth cleantech innovator targeting industrial wastewater treatment with a unique bio-electrochemical approach. It boasts a strong IP portfolio, improving margins, and expanding customer base. Significant market demand driven by environmental regulations and energy efficiency trends provide tailwinds. The company has delivered rapid revenue growth and is trending toward profitability and positive cash flow within the next 12 months.

This memo has been prepared for investment evaluation purposes.
reasoning:
Okay, looking at Ecolytix Technologies, they operate in industrial wastewater treatment with a cutting-edge bio-electrochemical reactor technology that seems quite innovative. Given the increasing pressure from environmental regulations globally, their cleantech solution targeting major manufacturing sectors is very timely.

Let me analyze their financials. They're showing impressive revenue growth, from €8.2M in 2022 to a forecasted €61.7M in 2026, which is roughly a 64% CAGR—very strong. Gross margins are improving steadily, now above 60%, which is good for a hardware plus service model.

EBITDA turned positive in 2023 and is projected to surpass 30% margin by 2026. Net income is also becoming positive, with improving cash generation and a net cash position projected by 2025. This suggests solid operational leverage and capital discipline.

The customer base includes sizable manufacturing companies with multi-year contracts featuring recurring revenue from monitoring and maintenance, improving revenue visibility and retention. The 95% reported retention is excellent, albeit customer concentration with top 5 clients at 50% revenue is a moderate risk.

Competition is present but Ecolytix differentiates itself with patented technology, modularity, and energy efficiencies. The digital platform for remote control and analytics is a nice value-added service that enhances stickiness.

Growth opportunities look robust: geographic expansion into North America underway, product pipeline with higher-capacity units, and recurring revenue mix expansion all bode well.

Risks include execution of new product scale-ups and regulatory shifts, but the recent €15M Series C funding shows investor confidence, supporting growth and innovation.

Overall, the company shows strong market positioning with defensible IP, compelling growth potential, improving unit economics, and a solid financial trajectory toward profitability and positive cash flow.

Given all this, I think Ecolytix represents a high-conviction investment opportunity with attractive risk-adjusted returns. I'd want to request some additional diligence on competitive benchmarking tech performance, detailed customer contracts and credit quality, and further granularity on R&D roadmap and regulatory scenario planning, but the signals are very strong for proceeding.

Hence, I would rate the investment signal as "strong_yes."

verdict.evidence_pro:
1. Proprietary and patented bio-electrochemical technology with modular, energy-efficient reactors differentiates Ecolytix in a growing industrial wastewater treatment market.
2. Rapid revenue growth at 64% CAGR with improving gross margins >60% and EBITDA margins trending towards 30% by 2026.
3. Strong recurring revenue streams from monitoring and maintenance contracts enhancing revenue visibility and client retention (>95%).
4. Expanding geographic footprint into North America and ongoing product pipeline innovation.
5. Recent €15M Series C funding supports scale-up and validates investor confidence.
6. Digital platform integration adds operational efficiency and client stickiness.
verdict.evidence_contra:
1. Customer concentration risk, with top 5 clients accounting for ~50% of revenues.
2. Execution risks related to scaling up new higher-capacity BER units and new catalyst formulations.
3. Potential regulatory shifts could adversely impact demand or require costly compliance.
4. Competition from established membrane bioreactors and emerging oxidation technologies.
5. Moderate capex requirements and working capital needs during rapid growth phase.
6. Market adoption risk in nascent North American region requiring successful commercial traction.
verdict.similar_deals:
• AquaVentures’ $120M growth equity investment in CleanStream BioSystems, a membrane bioreactor tech provider, 2023.
• GreenPeak Capital’s $85M buyout of EnviroTech Solutions, specializing in digital wastewater analytics and treatment, 2024.
• TerraFund’s strategic minority growth investment in BioPure Reactors, a bio-electrochemical wastewater startup, 2022.
• HydroGrid Partners' $60M Series B investment in ClearWave Technologies, cleantech membrane filtration, 2025.
verdict.key_information_requested:
• Detailed competitive performance benchmarking of Ecolytix’s BER technology vs membranes and oxidation alternatives including lifecycle costs and energy savings.
• Full customer contract terms, length, renewal rates, and creditworthiness analysis.
• Granular R&D pipeline milestones, burn rates, and timelines for new catalyst and modular unit innovations.
• Regulatory risk assessment scenarios including EU and US environmental policies' impact.
• Confirmation of intellectual property strength and freedom-to-operate.
• Sensitivity analysis on customer concentration and geographic expansion assumptions.
